sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
After the long start/finish straight, turn one is a heavy braking zone, requiring drivers to slow down from top speed, making it a key overtaking opportunity. Brake hard just after the 100m board, dont do what Valtteri Bottas did and take out half of the field at turn one, and change down to second gear. Aim to trail-brake slightly into the tight apex. The exit is critical – prioritise getting the car straight and on throttle early to maximise speed through the kink and into Turn two.

A tricky, off-camber corner that punishes poor line choice. You will enter slightly uphill, but will soon find yourself plummeting back downhill again mid-corner. Use a late apex and be patient with that right foot. Early entry will force you wide on exit and compromise turn three.

This fast right is essentially flat out in these modern Formula One cars. After exiting Turn 2, keep the car to the left and smoothly transition it into Turn 3. Focus on minimising steering input to avoid scrubbing speed, and line up nicely for the climb up the hill to turn four.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Blind and fast, Turn four is one of the most challenging corners on the track to get right lap after lap. Brake lightly and commit to a high-speed left entry, touching the inside curb, but not getting on top of it. It is easy to run wide on the exit, where gravel and track limits await. Confidence and precision are crucial here.

Downhill braking makes turn five somewhat deceptive. Make sure to brake earlier than you think you need to and avoid the inside kerb – it can unsettle the car. Get back on power early for the short sprint into the chicane. Focus on a clean, flowing exit to set up the following chicane.

A tight, bumpy section requiring precision. Brake hard and turn to the right, using a generous portion of the curb as a reference point. Immediately switch direction to the left – be cautious not to launch the car over the high kurb on the left. A smooth transition here sets up the flowing sequence ahead just right.

A fast rhythm section. Entry to turn eight must be clean – avoid overdriving. Use plenty of kerb, but keep the steering smooth. A slight lift or brake tap may be needed, depending on how your tyres are coping. The exit of Turn nine leads into a short straight, so lightning-fast throttle application is essential.

Turn ten is barely a kink, setting up the swift Turn eleven. Brake gently and turn in early, using the apex curb. This corner demands commitment – its fast, and the exit curb is bumpy.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
After the back straight, brake hard into this downhill right-hander for turn twelve. Aim for a late apex and avoid the kerbing on the inside. Let the car run out wide and use all the track. Prioritise a stable run through the corner over outright speed here, as it is easy to get it wrong.

This is a slow, tight corner that wraps around almost 180 degrees. Brake hard and stay tight to the apex, but be patient with the throttle – early power will push you wide. The exit grip is crucial for setting up the final corner.

A slightly cambered, medium-speed corner leading onto the pit straight. Brake late but smoothly, and aim for a somewhat late apex. Carry as much speed as possible onto the straight. A clean exit here is essential for setting up an overtake into turn one or getting the drive off the corner to defend from an attacking car.
